articles.title: WFP Sudan Country Brief May 2026
articles.description: Country: Sudan Source: World Food Programme Please refer to the attached file. On 2 April, the Office of the RC/HC relocated to Khartoum following the recent re-establishment of government ministries. WFP Sudan is implementing a phased return to Khartoum and rotations supported by the regular UNHAS airbridge. 15 April 2026 marked the third anniversary of the war in Sudan. In a statement , the UN Secretary‐General warned that Sudan has become the world’s largest humanitarian crisis and urged an immediate ceasefire, civilian protection, full funding for aid, and a credible civilian‐led political process. At the Berlin Conference on 15 April, donors pledged over USD 1.5 billion to Sudan. On 24 April, a truck carrying relief items from UNHCR came under drone attack in North Darfur, destroying emergency shelter supplies intended for 1,300 families in Tawila, where more than 700,000 displaced people have sought safety after fleeing fighting in other parts of Darfur.

articles.title: Tenderness and Rage: how groups affected by HIV found power, comfort and joy in Aids activism
articles.description: London exhibition explores how care and protest improved rights and dignity of those living with disease From photos of a mass “die-in” by Aids activists in Trafalgar Square, London, in the 1990s to plushie breasts, lips and vulvas hand-stitched by HIV-positive women, a new exhibition explores how care and protest have improved the rights and dignity of those living with the disease. The show, Tenderness and Rage , at the Wellcome Collection , London, reflects how different groups affected by HIV, including gay men, women of colour, and refugees in the UK and around the world have found power, solidarity, comfort and joy in Aids activism and support services. Continue reading...

articles.title: SCOTUS Justice Slams Colleagues as She Dissents Solo
articles.description: Jacquelyn Martin/Pool via Reuters Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson blasted her colleagues in a fierce dissent on Thursday over a decision limiting how federal prisoners can seek early release. Jackson, who was appointed by the U.S. Supreme Court by former President Joe Biden in 2022, was the sole dissenter to a decision that found that alleged invalidities of a conviction are not a valid basis for compassionate release. The court’s conservative majority, led by Justice Amy Coney Barrett, argued that federal prisoners cannot use compassionate release motions—which authorize district courts to permit reductions of federal prison sentences for “extraordinary and compelling reasons”—to challenge the validity of their convictions. Read more at The Daily Beast.

articles.title: How I Know Trump Is at His Most Dangerous: Biographer
articles.description: Photo Illustration by Victoria Sunday/The Daily Beast/Getty Images Donald Trump’s admission that he “doesn’t care” about the looming November midterms should be setting off alarm bells, his biographer warns. Michael Wolff said on the Inside Trump’s Head Podcast that the 79-year-old president’s comment indicates that he is scheming for ways to try to avoid the consequences of his actions. “They thought they were going to outwait me, you know. ‘We’ll outwait him, he’s got the midterms,’” Trump said at a Cabinet meeting on Wednesday. “I don’t care about the midterms. Look what happened last night; that was the prelude to the midterms.” Read more at The Daily Beast.

articles.title: Romualdez says no such thing as ‘functional control’ over budget
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — The camp of former House of Representatives Speaker Ferdinand Martin Romualdez has maintained that there is no such thing as a “functional control” over the entire budget process, noting that no single person has discretion over every step of appropriations. In a statement on Friday, Romualdez’s spokesperson lawyer Elaine Atienza said that they take exception to recent pronouncements that he was the mastermind of the flood control scam because he , as Speaker of the House during the 19th Congress, supposedly had “functional control.” It was Assistant Ombudsman Jose Dominic Clavano IV who made this statement about Romualdez, during a [...]... Keep on reading: Romualdez says no such thing as ‘functional control’ over budget
